title: Abort: stale exception in `cfunction_check_kwargs` (`methodobject.c:409`)
description: `sys._baserepl` (`Python/sysmodule.c:2620`) calls `PyRun_AnyFileExFlags`, ignores its return value, and unconditionally `Py_RETURN_NONE`s. Under OOM the REPL/parse machinery raises `MemoryError` that the interactive loop does not reliably clear, so `_baserepl` returns a non-NULL `None` with an exception still live; the next NOARGS `sys` builtin then trips the debug `assert(!_PyErr_Occurred(tstate))` in `cfunction_check_kwargs` and aborts.
notes: NOT free-threading-specific: a generic exception-discard bug in sys._baserepl. sys__baserepl_impl (sysmodule.c:2620) ignores the int return of PyRun_AnyFileExFlags and always returns None; under OOM the interactive loop (pythonrun.c:152-178) can return with a stale MemoryError, so _baserepl returns a non-NULL result with an exception set. Aborts on BOTH assertions-enabled builds (ft_debug_asan and jit); ft_release/upstream compile the assert out and propagate a clean MemoryError (exit 1), so they are n/a. Minimization is partial: the minimal stdlib repro (sys._baserepl(); sys._clear_type_cache() under set_nomemory(1,0), stdin at EOF) is deterministic but lands one frame earlier on _Py_CheckFunctionResult ('a function returned a result with an exception set', object type name MemoryError) because cfunction_vectorcall_NOARGS does not run the result check but the enclosing _PyObject_VectorcallTstate (pycore_call.h:145) does; the exact methodobject.c:409 frame is reproduced by the python-4/python-7 sys vehicles where the leak surfaces at the entry of the next NOARGS sys call. Fix: make sys__baserepl_impl return NULL when PyRun_AnyFileExFlags fails, and/or ensure _PyRun_InteractiveLoopObject never returns with a stale exception on the thread state.
